name: Satellite Overlay
author: TransitCore
description:
🛰 Satellite Overlay
See the real world beneath your subway network. This mod overlays live satellite
imagery directly on the game map, aligned to your city's actual geographic
coordinates — so you can plan routes along real streets, spot landmarks, and see
exactly where your stations land in the real city.
Toggle the satellite layer on and off, adjust opacity to blend it with the base map,
and hide individual map elements like buildings, water, or roads to get the exact
look you want.
Built using MapTiler's satellite tile API routed through a local proxy to work around
the game's network restrictions.

How to Install & Use
Before you start you'll need:
Node.js (LTS version — free)
A free MapTiler account

Step 1 — Create a MapTiler account
Go to cloud.maptiler.com and sign up for free. Once logged in, go to Account →
API Keys and copy your key. It looks something like n8sXxXXXXXXXXXXXf1.

Step 2 — Download the mod
Download and unzip the mod folder. Place it in your Subway Builder mods
directory:
Mac: ~/Library/Application Support/metro-maker4/mods/
Windows: %APPDATA%\metro-maker4\mods\
Then enable it in Settings → Mods inside the game.

Step 3 — Add your API key
Open proxy.js in a text editor and find line 22:
const KEY = 'YOUR_MAPTILER_KEY';
Replace YOUR_MAPTILER_KEY with the key you copied from MapTiler. For example if your tiles URL looks like:
https://api.maptiler.com/maps/satellite-v4/tiles.json?key=justthispartonly
Then you want just the part after key=

Step 4 — Run the proxy
Every time you want to download new tiles with the satellite overlay, open Terminal (Mac) or
Command Prompt (Windows) and run:
Mac: node ~/Library/Application\ Support/metro-maker4/mods/
satellite-overlay/proxy.js
Windows: node "%APPDATA%\metro-maker4\mods\satellite-overlay\proxy.js"

You should see:
Satellite tile proxy running
Tiles: http://127.0.0.1:8080/tile/{z}/{x}/{y}.jpg
Health: http://127.0.0.1:8080/health
Leave this terminal open the whole time you're playing. Press Ctrl+C to stop it when you're done.

Step 5 — Load a city
Launch Subway Builder and open any city. The satellite layer appears automatically. Click the Satellite toolbar button to open the control panel where you can:
-Toggle the overlay on/off
-Adjust opacity (blend satellite with the base map)
-Hide/show individual map layers (buildings, water, roads, parks, etc.)

Notes
1. Tile cache: Tiles are saved to a tile-cache/ folder inside the mod
directory after the first load. Revisiting the same area won't need a
network request, which means you only need to run the proxy when first loading a map.

2. Layer resets: Switching between 2D/3D view or opening windows like
construction costs or demand stats can cause tiles to temporarily
disappear. Just toggle any button in the satellite panel to restore them
instantly.

3. The proxy is required — the game's security policy blocks direct
connections to MapTiler, so the proxy runs locally to forward requests on
your behalf.
Tile data © MapTiler © OpenStreetMap contributors
